channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Haitians face record hunger as gang violence grips country in throes of economic crisis
item.description: A new report has found that more than half of Haiti’s population is expected to experience severe hunger through June, and another 8,400 people living in makeshift shelters are projected to starve.
item.dc:creator: Evens Sanon And Dánica Coto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump officials' defiance over Abrego Garcia's deportation is 'shocking,' appeals court says
item.description: A federal appeals court says it is “shocking” that The Trump administration claims it can’t do anything to free Kilmar Abrego Garcia from an El Salvador prison and return him to the U.S. A three-judge panel from the 4th U.S.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UN nuclear watchdog says US-Iran talks at a 'very crucial' stage
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Talks between Iran and the United States over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program are “in a very crucial” stage, the head of the United Nations' nuclear watchdog said Thursday while on a visit to the Islamic Republic.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and AMIR VAHDAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis visits Rome prison during Holy Week
item.description: Pope Francis kept annual Holy Week appointment visiting dozens of inmates in a Rome prison on Thursday. The 88-year-old is still recovering from pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Transgender women in Britain fear ruling could place toilets, sports and hospitals off limits
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Transgender women will be excluded from women’s toilets, hospital wards and sports teams after a U.K.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cable car accident in southern Italy kills at least 4 people
item.description: Officials in southern Italy say that a cable car carrying tourists south of Naples has crashed after the cable snapped, killing at least four people and critically injuring one.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: ICC opens inquiry into Hungary for failing to arrest Netanyahu
item.description: Judges at the International Criminal Court have asked Hungary to explain why it failed to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu when he visited Budapest earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Molly Quell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Funeral held in Kenya for TikTok content moderator following death in unclear circumstances
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — A funeral was held on Thursday for a TikTok content moderator who died in unclear circumstances in Kenya last month.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A Russian bucket brigade helps toads and frogs cross the road to get to a spawning site
item.description: A NATURE RESERVE NEAR SESTRORETSK, Russia (AP) — It happens every spring along a section of road north of Russia's second-largest city of St. Petersburg: Volunteers, many in yellow vests, patrol near the Sestroretsk Bog nature reserve.
item.dc:creator: KIRILL ZARUBIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Burchett signals support for sending US criminals to El Salvador
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett (R-Tenn.) on Wednesday defended President Trump’s suggestion that U.S. citizens convicted of violent crimes be sent to Salvadoran prisons. “They're criminals. They broke our laws.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Italian prime minister meets with Trump on tariffs
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni is expected to speak for Italy and the European Union as a whole.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Russia strikes Ukrainian city of Dnipro as Kyiv seeks security pledges in Paris
item.description: Ukrainian officials say Russian forces launched a massive drone attack on the southeastern Ukrainian city of Dnipro, killing three people, including a child.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Artisans in Guatemala hand-stitch velvet cloaks for Holy Week processions
item.description: SAN MIGUEL ESCOBAR, Guatemala (AP) — Stitch by stitch, artisans have worked for months on the elaborate garments that will debut this week in Holy Week processions across Guatemala and beyond.
item.dc:creator: SONIA PÉREZ D., Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea threatens a response to US flying long-range bombers over South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Thursday threatened unspecified retaliation after the U.S. flew long-range bombers over South Korea during training with South Korean warplanes, which North Korea views as practice for an attack against it. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli defense minister says troops will remain in Gaza, Lebanon and Syria indefinitely
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s defense minister said on Wednesday that troops will remain in so-called security zones in the Gaza Strip, Lebanon and Syria indefinitely, remarks that could further complicate talks with Hamas over a ceasefire and hostage release.
item.dc:creator: ISAAC SCHARF, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Dog reunites with Israeli family after disappearing for 18 months in Gaza
item.description: BINYAMINA, Israel (AP) — Rachel Dancyg never thought she would see her dog again after it disappeared in the Hamas attack that sparked the ongoing war with Israel. Her ex-husband and brother were abducted from Kibbutz Nir Oz in southern Israel and killed.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: WHO member states reach pandemic agreement
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) announced Wednesday its member states had reached an agreement on preparing for and responding to future pandemics after more than three years of negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: A Berlin doctor has been charged with the killings of 15 patients under palliative care
item.description: A German doctor has been charged with murdering over 15 people under palliative care; he has also been accused of attempting to cover up the crimes by starting fires in their homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look at recent global actions limiting legal recognitions for transgender people
item.description: A ruling Wednesday from the top court in the United Kingdom that says the legal definition of a woman is someone born biologically female is the latest high-profile action globally involving the issue of what legal recognitions transgender people are allowed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN envoy urges Security Council to try to prevent renewed civil war in South Sudan
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The top United Nations official in South Sudan urged the U.N. Security Council on Wednesday to use its clout to prevent the world’s newest nation from again plunging into civil war.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maryland man's family questions death, says he didn't drown in Bahamas
item.description: Dinari McAlmont of Bowie, Maryland, was found dead April 5 near the Atlantis resort on Paradise Island, according to Royal Bahamian Police.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: NATO chief reaffirms support for Ukraine during a visit to the port city of Odesa
item.description: ODESA, Ukraine (AP) — NATO’s support for Ukraine remains “unwavering,” the alliance’s secretary-general said Tuesday, emphasizing that more than 20 billion euros — over $22 billion — in security assistance has already been pledged by NATO allies in the first three months of the year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Four journalists who were accused of working for Kremlin foe Navalny are convicted of extremism
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Tuesday convicted four journalists of extremism for working for an anti-corruption group founded by the late opposition leader Alexei Navalny and sentenced them to 5 1/2 years in prison each.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas rejects Israeli ceasefire, hostage return deal over disarmament demands
item.description: Hamas has reportedly rejected Israel's latest ceasefire offer, which included the return of humanitarian aid in exchange for nearly a dozen hostages and Hamas' complete disarmament.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike outside a Gaza hospital kills a medic and wounds several
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit the northern gate of the Kuwaiti Field Hospital in the Gaza Strip on Tuesday, killing a medic and wounding nine other people — all patients and medics — according to a spokesman for the hospital in the Muwasi area, where hundreds of thousands
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US pastor abducted at gunpoint while preaching in South Africa
item.description: Four men stole two cellphones from members of the congregation and kidnapped the pastor in his own truck.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Father of American-Israeli hostage pushes for ceasefire
item.description: Adi Alexander shares a message for his son, urging him to stay strong and assuring him the family won’t stop fighting for his return.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Trump floats deporting 'homegrown criminals' to El Salvador
item.description: Trump has suggested shipping off U.S. citizens who commit violent crimes to places like El Salvador's notorious mega-prison CECOT.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: New report raises questions over the origins of COVID
item.description: A military report revealed that seven U.S. service members had COVID-like symptoms during an event in Wuhan in October of 2019.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, WGN 9

item.title: Trump hosts El Salvador's Nayib Bukele amid deportation controversy
item.description: Since March, El Salvador has accepted more than 200 Venezuelan immigrants accused by the U.S. of gang activity and violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis makes appearance after Palm Sunday mass at the Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is still recovering from double pneumonia, but the 88-year-old still met with faithful after the Palm Sunday mass at the Vatican on April 13, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu slams Carney's reply to anti-Israel agitator who said there's a 'genocide' in Gaza
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is demanding Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney "backtrack" his comments after the Canadian leader appeared to agree with a protester who said there's a "genocide" in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico to make ‘immediate’ water payment to US as Trump threatens sanctions
item.description: Hours after President Donald Trump threatened Mexico with additional tariffs over a massive water debt, that country's president publicly vowed to make a substantial payment soon.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Universal announces UK theme park amid 'epic' growth of parks business
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences is expanding its global theme park footprint, confirming a new theme park will be built in the U.K. -- the latest in a trio of ambitious projects for the company.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman unknowingly gives birth to a stranger's baby after IVF clinic error
item.description: A woman in Australia unknowingly gave birth to a stranger’s baby after she received another patient's embryo from her in vitro fertilization clinic due to “human error,” the clinic said.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump admin ordered to return alleged MS-13 member mistakenly deported
item.description: The justices did not say exactly what Kilmar Abrego Garcia's release would entail or how he would return to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation
